title: The Toad-Eater.
content:
What of earls with whom you have supt,
            And of dukes that you dined with yestreen?
        Lord! a louse, Sir, is still but a louse,
            Though it crawl on the curl of a queen.
meaning:
Robert Burns's contribution to classic is further solidified by the brilliance found in "The Toad-Eater."...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
